item.title: U.S., Iran have agreed to broad principles of deal, Trump official says
item.description: There is broad commitment on the principles of a deal, a senior Trump administration official said, and the administration feels positive about where things stand.

item.title: Iran agrees in principle to dispose of highly-enriched uranium, official says
item.description: But a deal to end the Iran war likely will not be signed this weekend, a senior Trump administration official said.

item.title: Reefline: Bringing art to Miami's coastline
item.description: Argentinian-born art curator Ximena Caminos is the mastermind behind Miami's newest art installation, Reefline, an underwater sculpture park that doubles as an artificial coral reef about 300 yards from the Florida coast and 20 feet below the surface. Luke Burbank reports.

item.title: Rubio says "significant progress" has been made in Iran talks, but not "final progress"
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Sunday that "significant progress" had been made in peace talks with Iran, but not "final progress" after President Trump's call with leaders of several Arab and Muslim countries. Imtiaz Tyab has the latest from Tel Aviv.

item.title: Transcript: Lt. Col. William Swenson (Ret.) and Command Sergeant Major Matthew Williams (Ret.) on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," May 24, 2026
item.description: The following is the transcript of the interview with retired Lt. Col. William Swenson and retired Command Sergeant Major Matthew Williams (Ret.) that aired on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on May 24, 2026.

item.title: Full interview: Lt. Col. William Swenson (Ret.) and Master Sgt. Matthew Williams (Ret.)
item.description: Watch Margaret Brennan's full interview with Medal of Honor recipients Retired Army Lt. Col. William Swenson and retired Army Command Sergeant Major Matthew Williams, a portion of which aired on May 24, 2026.

item.title: Celebrating most prestigious, and junkers, of automobile world
item.description: In California, the Pebble Beach Concours d'Elegance is widely considered the most prestigious car show in the world. But just a few miles away, the Concours d'Lemons celebrates the junkers of the automotive world.

item.title: Celebrating the most prestigious and the ugliest of the automotive world
item.description: In California, the Pebble Beach Concours d'Elegance is an invite-only event celebrating the most valuable collector cars in the world. And just a few miles away, Concours d'LeMons is a once-a-year celebration of the less desirable, less loved and less expensive automobiles. Lee Cowan heads to both events — one that celebrates the best in show, and the other only a worst in show.

item.title: Open: This is "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," May 24, 2026
item.description: This week on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," as President Trump says a peace deal with Iran has been "largely negotiated," Imtiaz Tyab reports from Tel Aviv and Sen. Chris Van Hollen discusses the possible deal. Plus, on this Memorial weekend, Medal of Honor recipients retired Lt. Col. William Swenson and retired Master Sgt. Matthew Williams join.

item.title: Nature: Seals in Maine
item.description: We leave you this Sunday with seals (at rest) near Mount Desert Island in northern Maine. Videographer: Mauricio Handler.

item.title: Douglas McCain, the eldest son of Sen. John McCain, dies at 66
item.description: Douglas McCain, the eldest son of the late Sen. John McCain, has died, his family announced. He was 66.

item.title: These United States: The Marshall Plan
item.description: In 1947, two years after the end of World War II, President Truman's secretary of state, George C. Marshall, delivered a commencement speech arguing the U.S. should help its European allies and enemies rebuild in the post-war world. This was the foundation of an economic aid program that came to be known as the Marshall Plan. Seth Doane looks at its monumental impact.

item.title: Trump touts a potential agreement with Iran
item.description: President Trump said Saturday on social media that an agreement between the U.S. and Iran "has been largely negotiated" after weeks of diplomacy. However, some Republicans have warned Mr. Trump that Iran can't be trusted. Robert Costa has the latest.

item.title: Passage: In memoriam
item.description: On Memorial Day, "Sunday Morning" remembers the fallen, including the U.S. service members killed since the start of the war with Iran.

item.title: Almanac: May 24
item.description: "Sunday Morning" looks back at historical events on this date.

item.title: 2 dead, dozens wounded after Russia launches large attack on Ukraine
item.description: Russia used a powerful hypersonic missile that has nuclear capabilities to carry out a massive attack Sunday in Kyiv, Ukraine.

item.title: Recipe: Lavender and lemon poppy seed cake from "Cake Picnic"
item.description: Elisa Sunga shares how to make lavender and lemon poppy seed cake in her cookbook, "Cake Picnic: Recipes for the Love of Cake & Friends."

item.title: 24 killed, dozens more injured in suicide bombing in Pakistan
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif called the attack a "cowardly act of terrorism" on social media.

item.title: Bob Dylan on what New York City means to him | 60 Minutes Archive
item.description: In 2004, Bob Dylan spoke with 60 Minutes' Ed Bradley about the role New York City played in his life. In this expanded clip from the interview outtakes, the singer-songwriter discusses his journey from Minnesota to New York. Today, the musician turns 85.

item.title: Second Ebola treatment center set on fire in epicenter of disease's outbreak
item.description: Angry residents of a town at the epicenter of the Ebola outbreak in eastern Congo​ attacked and burned a tent that was part of a health center where people are being treated for the virus.

item.title: Gunman killed after opening fire on Secret Service checkpoint outside White House
item.description: A bystander was also wounded, but no Secret Service officers were injured, officials said. President Trump was in the White House at the time of the incident.

item.title: California Gov. Newsom declares state of emergency for Orange County chemical leak as DA launches probe into its cause
item.description: Southern California officials are desperately searching for a safe resolution to a leaking toxic chemical tank at an aerospace facility.

item.title: Why Kouri Richins jurors decided Utah mom was a killer
item.description: At Kouri Richins' sentencing for the murder of Eric Richins, her husband and father of their three sons, she declared her innocence to the court and to her children. But for jurors on the case, the evidence told a different story.

item.title: "48 Hours" show schedule
item.description: True crime. Real justice. To miss it would be a crime.

item.title: Why did Kouri Richins want her husband dead?
item.description: Kouri Richins published a children's book to help her sons cope with the loss of their father – then she was convicted of Eric Richins' murder.

item.title: Iran moving World Cup training base from U.S. to Mexico
item.description: Iran had been scheduled to train in Tucson, Arizona, but it will now move its training base to Tijuana, Mexico, just south of San Diego.

item.title: Russia launches heavy missile strikes on Kyiv after Ukraine drone attack
item.description: A large ballistic missile attack pounded Kyiv, authorities said, wounding at least five people after Moscow threatened retaliation for strikes in Russian-occupied eastern Ukraine.

item.title: Serbian protesters clash with police amid anti-government rally in Belgrade
item.description: Protesters on Saturday streamed into central Belgrade, many carrying banners and wearing T-shirts inscribed with the "Students win" motto of the youth movement.

item.title: Kouri Richins: Behind the Facade
item.description: Scared of their own mother. Why the children of convicted killer Kouri Richins want her to stay behind bars. "48 Hours" correspondent Natalie Morales reports.

item.title: SpaceX launches more powerful Super Heavy-Starship rocket
item.description: Perfecting SpaceX's mammoth rocket will be critical to NASA's plans for returning astronauts to the surface of the moon.
